methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $130M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Infrastructure & Logistics (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Infrastructure & Logistics, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Amperon
private.rationale: Same sector (Infrastructure & Logistics), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Earth AI, a Series B infrastructure and logistics company founded by Roman Teslyuk, has raised $20M to date. The comparable universe for this sector is limited; while two private peers exist (AiDash and Amperon), no public comparables are available, and the narrow peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or similar-tier datapoints to support a reliable peer-median valuation method.

The stage-progression method is applicable here. Using total capital raised ($20M) as a proxy baseline and anchoring to $130M, a typical Series B-to-next-stage multiplier of 1.9x yields an implied valuation of $247M. This method reflects observed step-up patterns for companies at this funding stage, though it carries inherent model risk given the nascent comparable data for infrastructure tech.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $210M (low) to $284M (high), centered on $247M. This range reflects a single methodological input and should be weighted accordingly. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the stage-progression multiplier for early-stage infrastructure plays, the limited peer visibility in this sector, and the absence of market-tested valuations from comparable rounds. A Series B transaction would benefit from direct market testing given the thinness of public benchmarking data.
